article_meta.title: President Donald Trump’s mass deportation operation is expected to ramp up in 2026 as newly hired military lawyers-turne

reasoning: Mostly neutral paraphrase with appropriate attribution of critiques; minor inheritance of loaded terms like "mass deportation" and an un-attributed phrase "pushing out" introduce limited framing and speculation.

custom_reasoning.spin: Generally neutral verbs, but "pushing out" and "cadre" add interpretive tone; "mass deportation" echoes headline framing.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States that hundreds of JAG judges "will be brought in" and that judges were "pushed out" without firm attribution or evidence beyond criticism.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Minor: presents "pushing out" as fact; otherwise evaluative claims are attributed to critics.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic language beyond limited loaded terms; tone remains measured.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims of knowing intentions; critical claims about incentives are attributed.
custom_reasoning.slant: Covers admin goals and critics; slight emphasis on critical concerns about legality and neutrality.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes both supportive and critical perspectives; no major viewpoint missing.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Sparse use; "significantly" and "mass"/"cadre" are mildly subjective.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Avoids politically charged terms like "illegal immigrants"; "mass deportation" and "cadre" carry some charge.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Some focus on denials and legal concerns, but balanced with procedural aims.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and expert sources similarly; no clear deference.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than source overall but retains a few loaded phrases.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Both administration/supporters and critics (immigration attorneys/researchers) are represented, but critics get slightly more space.
